perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be one (1) year unless terminated earlier in accordance with the terms of this Agreement (the "Term").
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall be five (5) years unless terminated earlier in accordance with the terms of this Agreement (the "Term").
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the initial term of the agreement from one (1) year to five (5) years. This directly contradicts the early termination clause in Section 21, which is written with the assumption of the agreement having a shorter, one-year duration. This creates uncertainty about when the obligations related to termination become valid, considering the potential for a five-year term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties may terminate its performance of related obligations under this Agreement within thirty (30) days of receipt by the Party of written termination notice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the agreement's term from one year to five years. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which allows termination within thirty (30) days of receiving written termination notice. The original one-year term provides a context for the termination clause, whereas the new five-year term significantly alters the expected timeline and obligation, creating uncertainty and potentially leading to disputes. If the agreement was expected to last one-year, a 30 day notice period makes logical sense; however, if the agreement is expected to last five years, it makes the termination clause incompatible without modification, because it was written with the assumption of the agreement having a shorter, one-year duration. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.'

perturbation.original_text: The Parties are working cooperatively to develop and cooperate to expand the GINARES projects. The Parties agree to develop a list of target cooperation projects and common goals, and consequent agreements if required, within six (6) Months of signing this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties are working cooperatively to develop and cooperate to expand the GINARES projects. The Parties agree to develop a list of target cooperation projects and common goals, and consequent agreements if required, within twelve (12) Months of signing this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by extending the deadline for developing a list of target cooperation projects from six (6) months to twelve (12) months. This contradicts the term described in Section 1, making unclear if the extension applies to the original one year period of the agreement, or creates an extension to the entire contract's duration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement shall be one (1) year unless terminated earlier in accordance with the terms of this Agreement (the "Term").
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the deadline for developing a list of cooperation projects from six months to twelve months. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states the agreement's term is one year. If the list of projects isn't developed until twelve months, it conflicts with the one-year term of the contract, causing uncertainty about project implementation or the contract's duration.
